title: SSRF in SendEmailBlock via user-controlled SMTP server/port bypassing the IP blocklist
description: SendEmailBlock in autogpt_platform/backend/backend/blocks/email_block.py accepts a user-supplied smtp_server (string) and smtp_port (int) as per-execution block inputs (SMTPConfig embedded in Input) and passes them directly into smtplib.SMTP(smtp_server, smtp_port, timeout=30) to open a raw TCP connection with no IP validation. This bypasses the platform's hardened SSRF protections in backend/util/request.py (validate_url_host / BLOCKED_IP_NETWORKS) that every other outbound-connecting block uses. An authenticated user can target internal IPs/ports (e.g. 10.0.0.1:22, 169.254.169.254), and because smtplib reads the target's TCP banner on connect and embeds it in exception messages that are persisted as user-visible block output, this enables non-blind internal port scanning and service fingerprinting.
